title: Exclude investigator beads from automatic followup creation
description: Update scheduler followup logic so completed investigator beads never spawn tester, documentation, or review followups, while preserving normal conflict-scope behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Followup creation short-circuits for investigator beads alongside other excluded agent types.
Scheduler tests prove an investigator bead can complete without `-test`, `-docs`, or `-review` children.
Existing developer followup behavior remains unchanged.

handoff_summary.completed: Added an explicit comment in `_create_followups` listing all excluded agent types (including investigator, review, tester, documentation, recovery, planner, scheduler). Added `test_investigator_bead_does_not_spawn_followups` to `test_scheduler_followups.py` — it creates an investigator bead, runs the scheduler, asserts the bead reaches DONE status, and asserts no -test, -docs, or -review children were created.
handoff_summary.remaining: N/A — both acceptance criteria that involve this bead are satisfied: (1) code documents the exclusion, (2) test proves investigator beads complete without followups, (3) existing developer followup behavior is untouched.
handoff_summary.risks: None. The code change is additive (comment only); no behavioral change was needed because the `bead.agent_type != "developer"` guard already excluded investigator. The new test is isolated and does not touch shared fixtures.
handoff_summary.next_action: Run the tester bead to validate the new test passes and no regressions exist in the followups test suite.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Low — both changed files are within the declared scope of this bead and do not overlap with other in-progress beads.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: No behavioral change was required: the existing `if bead.agent_type != "developer": return created` guard in `_create_followups` already prevents investigator beads (and all other non-developer types) from getting auto-followups. The code change is limited to a comment that enumerates every excluded type — including investigator explicitly — so the intent is self-documenting. An alternative would have been to introduce a `FOLLOWUP_EXCLUDED_TYPES` set, but that would be a premature abstraction for a single-guard check.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: The tester should verify: (1) `test_investigator_bead_does_not_spawn_followups` passes — bead reaches DONE with zero -test, -docs, -review children; (2) no regressions in `test_scheduler_creates_followup_beads_for_developer` and surrounding developer followup tests; (3) edge case: investigator bead inside a planner-owned feature tree also produces no followups (not covered by the new test, but the guard is unconditional so the logic holds).
handoff_summary.known_limitations: The new test does not exercise an investigator bead nested inside a planner-owned feature tree. Given the guard is unconditional (`agent_type != "developer"`), the behaviour is correct regardless, but a follow-up bead could add that coverage if needed.

conflict_risks: Low — both changed files are within the declared scope of this bead and do not overlap with other in-progress beads.

execution_history.summary: Excluded investigator beads from automatic followup creation by documenting the exclusion explicitly in followups.py and adding a scheduler test proving an investigator bead completes without spawning -test, -docs, or -review children.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
